text: As I stand before this R+D exhibition, a subtle disorientation washes over me—spaces reflected and refracted in glass, the hint of technological promise hanging in the air. The play of light and color on the walls makes me think about innovation and the shifting boundary between observer and participant. I feel a curious intrigue, aware of my role as both an experiencer and a thinker. There is an openness in the ambiguity—a gentle invitation to let meaning emerge slowly, rather than rushing to solidify it.
